theme: Build a min-multiply evaluator for a fixed polynomial
title: Depressed-Square Evaluator: Minimum-Multiplication Straight-Line Programs
strategies: textbook Horner's rule on the raw coefficients, n multiplications (greedy)
recover the planted completing-the-square shift b/2, Taylor-shift the coefficients, evaluate y=(x+b/2)^2 then Horner in y, n/2+1 multiplications (strong)
the true minimum multiplication count for a fixed general polynomial is an open problem in algebraic complexity (open ceiling, uncovered)
